 {"id":"bd-1zk1v","title":"Task: OpenAPI contract tests + schema stability","description":"## Scope\nEnsure OpenAPI spec stability and contract correctness in CI.\n\n## Implementation\n- Generate openapi.json in CI.\n- Compare against checked‑in spec (oasdiff or Go diff).\n- Validate schema examples exist for all endpoints.\n\n## Logging\n- Log diff summary on failure.\n\n## Tests\n- Contract test job in CI pipeline.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- CI fails on any spec drift.\n- Spec generation is deterministic.\n","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:54:47.460395873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:15:03.900610047Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zk1v","depends_on_id":"bd-vmwdj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:15:03.900575792Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1zrjo","title":"TOON: Implement serialization wrapper supporting JSON and TOON","description":"Create abstraction layer for robot mode output serialization.

## Requirements

1. Single interface for serialization
2. Support JSON and TOON backends
3. Runtime switchable (per-command or global)
4. Handle errors gracefully (fallback to JSON)

## Implementation

```go
package serialize

type Format string

const (
    FormatJSON Format = "json"
    FormatTOON Format = "toon"
)

type Serializer interface {
    Serialize(v any) ([]byte, error)
    ContentType() string
}

func NewSerializer(format Format) Serializer {
    switch format {
    case FormatTOON:
        return &TOONSerializer{}
    default:
        return &JSONSerializer{}
    }
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/serialize/serialize.go
- internal/serialize/json.go
- internal/serialize/toon.go

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Serializer interface defined with Serialize() and ContentType() methods
2. JSONSerializer implements interface using encoding/json
3. TOONSerializer implements interface calling toon binary
4. NewSerializer factory function returns correct implementation
5. Fallback to JSON when toon binary not found
6. Error wrapping provides clear context

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/serialize/serialize_test.go
- Test round-trip JSON encoding/decoding
- Test TOON encoding with mock binary
- Test fallback behavior when toon not available

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-SERIALIZER] Serialization Wrapper Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-SERIALIZER]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing serialization wrapper"
# Test JSON serialization works
TEST_DATA='{"key":"value"}'
echo "$TEST_DATA" | go run ./cmd/serialize-test/ --format json | jq -e '.' || exit 1
echo "$LOG_PREFIX JSON serialization passed"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Serialization wrapper supports JSON and TOON
2. Same Go types work with both formats
3. TOON output 40-60 percent smaller than JSON
4. Round-trip parsing verified
5. Unit tests for both formats
6. Benchmark comparing sizes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:03:31.235798416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:13:18.555102773Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zrjo","depends_on_id":"bd-1u462","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:05:34.920279620Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zrjo","depends_on_id":"bd-6wc0a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:03:31.300335320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zrjo","depends_on_id":"bd-77bxi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:05:15.973313400Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1zs9y","title":"Feature: Token Budget Advisor","description":"## Summary
Estimate token usage for selected modes and provide budget-aware recommendations before running an ensemble.

## Rationale
Token costs can be significant; users need visibility and alternatives.

## Implementation
- Per-mode estimation using token heuristics (bd-2t4cs) + historical calibration.
- `ntm ensemble estimate` shows per-mode breakdown + total estimate.
- `--budget` flag warns and suggests cheaper alternatives.
- Track actual vs. estimated to improve calibration over time.

## Logging
- Log estimates, calibration deltas, and budget warnings.

## Tests
- Unit: estimation accuracy + calibration adjustment
- Integration: budget warnings with sample configs
- E2E: estimate output + budget warning path

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reasonably accurate estimates
- [ ] Per-mode breakdown available
- [ ] Budget warnings fire at thresholds
- [ ] Alternative mode suggestions
- [ ] Calibration improves over time

## Dependencies
- Budget control + tracking (bd-1dt84, bd-3d4tl)
- Token estimation heuristics (bd-2t4cs)
- Mode catalog (bd-11mtt)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:09:08.646964343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:47:34.424225995Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:05:53.961633398Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-1dt84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:05:23.775687234Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:12:35.919654679Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-2t4cs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:05:43.943288184Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-3d4tl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:05:33.644604912Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-204ic","title":"Task: REST core endpoints (system, sessions, panes, agents)","description":"## Scope\nImplement REST endpoints for system health/config and session/agent/pane management.\n\n## Endpoints (per Opus plan)\n- System: /health, /version, /deps, /config (GET/PATCH), /doctor.\n- Sessions: list/create/get/kill/spawn/quick/attach/view/zoom/snapshot/watch.\n- Panes: list/get/input/interrupt/output/capture/title.\n- Agents: list/add/send/interrupt/wait/route/activity/health/context/rotate.\n\n## Logging\n- Log session + pane + agent identifiers in every handler.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: handler validation + error codes.\n- Integration: spawn session, send prompt, verify output tail.\n- E2E (CLI parity): REST results match robot outputs.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- All listed endpoints respond with robot‑style envelopes.\n- Error codes match robot mode (SESSION_NOT_FOUND, PANE_NOT_FOUND, etc.).\n- Parity tests pass.\n","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:54.889532345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:04:36.725967407Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-204ic","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:04:24.103835148Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-204ic","depends_on_id":"bd-yn7qe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:04:36.725925257Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-204ic","title":"Task: REST core endpoints (system, sessions, panes, agents)","description":"## Scope\nImplement REST endpoints for system health/config and session/agent/pane management.\n\n## Endpoints (per Opus plan)\n- System: /health, /version, /deps, /config (GET/PATCH), /doctor.\n- Sessions: list/create/get/kill/spawn/quick/attach/view/zoom/snapshot/watch.\n- Panes: list/get/input/interrupt/output/capture/title.\n- Agents: list/add/send/interrupt/wait/route/activity/health/context/rotate.\n\n## Logging\n- Log session + pane + agent identifiers in every handler.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: handler validation + error codes.\n- Integration: spawn session, send prompt, verify output tail.\n- E2E (CLI parity): REST results match robot outputs.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- All listed endpoints respond with robot‑style envelopes.\n- Error codes match robot mode (SESSION_NOT_FOUND, PANE_NOT_FOUND, etc.).\n- Parity tests pass.\n","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:54.889532345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T18:29:05.129626471Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-204ic","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:04:24.103835148Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-204ic","depends_on_id":"bd-yn7qe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:04:36.725925257Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-209g","title":"Add Agent Mail integration - auto-register agents with MCP Agent Mail on spawn","description":"## Overview
Auto-register spawned agents with MCP Agent Mail for coordination.

## Implementation
When agents spawn, NTM:
1. Calls `mcp__mcp-agent-mail__ensure_project` with workspace path
2. For each agent, calls `mcp__mcp-agent-mail__register_agent` with:
   - program: 'claude-code' | 'codex-cli' | 'gemini-cli'
   - model: agent model
   - task_description: from spawn config or 'General development'

## Agent Names
Uses adjective+noun format per Agent Mail rules (e.g., 'GreenCastle', 'BlueLake').
Maps pane -> agent name for later reference.

## Error Handling
```go
type AgentMailError struct {
    Phase    string  // "ensure_project", "register_agent", "health_check"
    AgentIdx int     // Which agent failed (-1 for project-level)
    Cause    error
}
```

### Scenarios
1. **Agent Mail server not running**
   - Detect via health_check timeout (2s)
   - Log warning: "Agent Mail unavailable, skipping registration"
   - Continue spawn without registration
   - Set session flag: `agent_mail_available = false`

2. **ensure_project fails**
   - Log warning with error details
   - Continue spawn without registration
   - Retry on next operation

3. **register_agent fails for some agents**
   - Log warning for each failed agent
   - Continue with other agents
   - Return partial success in output

4. **Name collision**
   - Agent Mail auto-generates unique name
   - Store whatever name is returned

## Fallback Mode
When Agent Mail unavailable:
- File reservations disabled (warn user)
- Agent coordination disabled
- ntm assign still works (just no Agent Mail features)

## Output
```json
{
  "agent_mail": {
    "available": true,
    "project_registered": true,
    "agents_registered": 4,
    "agents_failed": 0,
    "agent_map": {
      "2": "GreenCastle",
      "3": "BlueLake",
      "4": "RedStone",
      "5": "PurpleBear"
    }
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Registers all agents on spawn
- [ ] Uses correct adjective+noun names
- [ ] Maps pane numbers to agent names
- [ ] Handles server unavailable gracefully (warning, continue)
- [ ] Handles partial registration failures
- [ ] Stores agent names in session state
- [ ] Falls back gracefully when unavailable
- [ ] Clear logging for all error scenarios

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Agent name generation
- Unit test: Pane-to-name mapping
- Unit test: Error handling for each scenario
- E2E test: Registration with real Agent Mail
- E2E test: Graceful degradation when unavailable","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:36:38.950986689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:58:35.143387294Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:58:35.143294539Z","close_reason":"Implemented Agent Mail auto-registration on spawn. Added registerSpawnedAgents function in spawn.go that: (1) Ensures project exists via EnsureProject, (2) Creates unique agent identities for each spawned agent using CreateAgentIdentity, (3) Maps pane index to agent name, (4) Returns status in SpawnResponse JSON with available, project_registered, agents_registered, agents_failed, and agent_map fields. Also fixed pre-existing bugs: orphaned code in FormatRecoveryPrompt and missing UsagePercent field access in rotation.go.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-209g","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:50:28.660287827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-20kv","title":"Add smart agent selection - match bead type/tags to agent strengths (Claude for architecture, Codex for boilerplate)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:39:48.859658892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:23:14.643777821Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T18:23:14.643733157Z","close_reason":"Over-engineering: agent strength matching requires maintaining model capability profiles and bead type mappings. User judgment about agent assignment is more flexible and accurate.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-20ong","title":"Task: Add robot pagination (limit/offset + next_cursor)","description":"## Overview
Prevent huge robot outputs by adding standard pagination fields.

## Behavior
- Common flags: `--robot-limit`, `--robot-offset`
- Output includes `next_cursor` or `has_more`
- Applies to commands returning large arrays (status, snapshot, history)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Limits reduce token usage deterministically
- Output indicates whether more data is available
- Works for JSON and TOON","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:17:02.311022877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T04:36:11.770734114Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20ong","depends_on_id":"bd-2dovu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:17:02.339713806Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-20ong","depends_on_id":"bd-2q3cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:17:09.009777764Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
